message.content:
You are implementing one commit in a Rust project at /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1. Create a new module and wire it in. DO NOT commit, DO NOT push, DO NOT change version numbers or notes files. Report what you changed.

# Goal
Add a single code-declared **config schema registry**: the source of truth enumerating every settable config key across vc-x1's two config homes. Later commits (a `config` print command, init's commented defaults, a `--validate` check) will all derive from this so they cannot drift. THIS commit adds only the registry + tests; no user-visible behavior yet.

# Deliverable: new file src/config_schema.rs
Module docstring (`//!`) explaining it's the single source of truth for settable config keys. Follow the project's doc-comment convention (every item gets a `///` doc comment; prose form = short intro + bullets).

Define:
```rust
/// Which config home a key belongs to.
/// - `User` — ~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml
/// - `WorkspaceCode` / `WorkspaceBot` — <root>/.vc-config.toml in
///   the work repo vs the .claude bot repo
pub enum Home { User, WorkspaceCode, WorkspaceBot }

/// The value shape a key expects (for rendering / future typed use).
pub enum ValueKind { Str, Usize, ItemList }

/// One settable configuration key.
pub struct ConfigKey {
    pub path: &'static str,          // dotted TOML path, e.g. "bot-session.col-width"
    pub homes: &'static [Home],      // which home(s) accept it
    pub kind: ValueKind,
    pub default: Option<&'static str>, // rendered default, None if no default
    pub required: bool,              // active (not commented) in init; role-specific value
    pub dynamic: bool,               // path has a <placeholder> segment (matches a family of keys)
    pub doc: &'static str,           // one-line description
}

/// The complete registry of settable keys.
pub fn schema() -> &'static [ConfigKey];
```
Derive `Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq` on the enums as appropriate. `schema()` returns a `&'static [ConfigKey]` backed by a `const` or `static` array.

# The keys to enumerate (EXACT — this is the whole surface)

User home (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`):
| path | homes | kind | default | required | dynamic | doc |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `default.account` | [User] | Str | None | false | false | Default account when --account is absent |
| `default.debug` | [User] | Str | None | false | false | Default --debug value when used without an argument (reserved; not yet consumed) |
| `repo.default` | [User] | Str | None | false | false | Top-level shorthand: repo category to use when --repo is absent |
| `repo.category.<cat>` | [User] | Str | None | false | true | Top-level shorthand: literal value for repo category <cat> |
| `account.<name>.repo.default` | [User] | Str | None | false | true | Per-account default repo category |
| `account.<name>.repo.category.<cat>` | [User] | Str | None | false | true | Per-account literal value for repo category <cat> |

bot-session keys — accepted in ALL THREE homes ([User, WorkspaceCode, WorkspaceBot]):
| path | kind | default | required | dynamic | doc |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `bot-session.items` | ItemList | Some("headers,user,assistant,tool,summary") | false | false | Default bot-session item set (comma-separated) |
| `bot-session.result-lines` | Usize | Some("10") | false | false | Default --result-lines: max lines shown per tool result (0 = unlimited) |
| `bot-session.col-width` | Usize | Some("68") | false | false | Default --col-width: first-column width in the field-inventory views |

Workspace home only ([WorkspaceCode, WorkspaceBot]):
| path | kind | default | required | dynamic | doc |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `workspace.path` | Str | None | true | false | This repo's path relative to the workspace root (role-specific: "/" for the work repo, "/.claude" for the bot repo) |
| `workspace.other-repo` | Str | None | false | false | Relative path to the counterpart repo; presence signals dual-repo mode (role-specific: ".claude" for the work repo, ".." for the bot repo) |
| `push.state-dir` | Str | Some(".vc-x1") | false | false | Directory (relative to repo root) holding the push state file |
| `push.state-file` | Str | Some("push-state.toml") | false | false | Filename of the push state file under push.state-dir |

Notes:
- `workspace.path` / `workspace.other-repo` have role-specific values, so their `default` is `None` here (the doc records the per-role values; init will emit the concrete values by role in a later commit). `workspace.path` is `required: true`.
- The three `bot-session.*` keys appear ONCE each with `homes: &[Home::User, Home::WorkspaceCode, Home::WorkspaceBot]`.

# Drift-protection unit tests (in src/config_schema.rs #[cfg(test)])
The numeric/string defaults must stay in lockstep with their source constants. To let the test reference them, change these four constants to `pub(crate)` (add `pub(crate)` — do not move or rename them):
- `src/bot_session.rs`: `const COL_WIDTH` (=68) and `const RESULT_LINE_CAP` (=10) → `pub(crate) const ...`
- `src/push.rs`: `const DEFAULT_STATE_DIR` (".vc-x1") and `const DEFAULT_STATE_FILE` ("push-state.toml") → `pub(crate) const ...`

Then tests:
- `defaults_match_source_consts`: assert the schema's `bot-session.col-width` default == `crate::bot_session::COL_WIDTH.to_string()`, `bot-session.result-lines` == `crate::bot_session::RESULT_LINE_CAP.to_string()`, `push.state-dir` == `crate::push::DEFAULT_STATE_DIR`, `push.state-file` == `crate::push::DEFAULT_STATE_FILE`. (Find each key in `schema()` by `path`.)
- `paths_unique`: no two `ConfigKey`s share a `path`.
- `homes_non_empty`: every key has at least one `Home`.
- `dynamic_keys_have_placeholder`: every key with `dynamic == true` contains a `<` in its `path`, and every key WITHOUT a `<` has `dynamic == false`.

# Wire-in
- Add `mod config_schema;` to the `mod` block at the top of `src/main.rs` (keep it in the existing alphabetical-ish order near `mod config;`).
- The module is currently unused by non-test code, which will trip `dead_code` under clippy -D warnings. Handle it cleanly: add `#[allow(dead_code)]` on the `config_schema` module items (or the `mod` declaration) with a short `// OK:`-style note that the consumers land in the following commits. Prefer allowing at the module or item level over leaving warnings.

# Validate (must all pass)
- `cargo fmt`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test`
Fix and re-run until green. Avoid `.unwrap()`/`.expect()` in non-test code (project lints them); tests may use them.

Report: the final `ConfigKey` list you encoded (paths only is fine), the exact enum/struct definitions, which consts you made pub(crate), how you handled the dead_code lint, and confirmation fmt/clippy/test are green.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact page can be granted — behavior static HTML cannot provide on its own, such as the page reading live or connected data, keeping state shared across viewers, or updating and republishing itself. Serves this user's live capability roster and the typed call definitions. Load it whenever the user asks for an artifact needing any such runtime behavior.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- claude-in-chrome: Automates your Chrome browser to interact with web pages - clicking elements, filling forms, capturing screenshots, reading console logs, and navigating sites. Opens pages in new tabs within your existing Chrome session. Requires site-level permissions before executing (configured in the extension). - When the user wants to interact with web pages, automate browser tasks, capture screenshots, read console logs, or perform any browser-based actions. Always invoke BEFORE attempting to use any mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tools.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	//! The `bot-session` subcommand: display a Claude Code session
2	//! transcript (`.jsonl`) as a readable conversation.
3	//!
4	//! Output is a set of *items* — headers, user, assistant, tool,
5	//! thinking, results, meta, summary — each toggled by `--<item>` /
6	//! `--no-<item>` flags (last one wins), with `--all` / `--none` as
7	//! bulk bases. The default set (headers, user, assistant, tool,
8	//! summary) can be replaced by the user config's
9	//! `[bot-session].items` list; CLI flags then adjust the resolved
10	//! set. Bookkeeping entry types are never rendered.
11	//! A trailing summary line always reports what was hidden or
12	//! skipped. Malformed lines (e.g. a live session's truncated
13	//! last line) warn to stderr and never fail the run.
14	
15	use std::path::PathBuf;
16	
17	use clap::Args;
18	use log::{debug, info, warn};
19	use serde_json::Value;
20	
21	use crate::context::Context;
22	use crate::subcommand::SubcommandRunner;
23	use crate::transcript::{self, ContentBlock, EntryKind, FileTranscript};
24	
25	/// Default max lines of one tool result shown under `--results`.
26	const RESULT_LINE_CAP: usize = 10;
27	
28	/// Default first-column (dotted-path) width in the `--fields` /
29	/// `--unknown` / `--per-line` views.
30	///
31	/// - 68 aligns the type column for ~99% of observed key paths —
32	///   every structural key except a long tail of
33	///   `snapshot.trackedFileBackups.<absolute path>.*` keys, whose
34	///   embedded absolute paths can be arbitrarily long and so are
35	///   left to overflow.
36	/// - Override with `--col-width`.
37	const COL_WIDTH: usize = 68;
38	
39	/// Max chars of a tool-use one-liner gist.
40	const GIST_CHAR_CAP: usize = 100;
41	
42	/// Clap-derived args for `bot-session`.
43	#[derive(Args, Debug)]
44	pub struct BotSessionArgs {
45	    /// Session transcript .jsonl file to display

255	
256	/// Current state-file format version — bump when the flat key set
257	/// changes incompatibly so readers can detect stale state and refuse
258	/// to resume instead of silently misinterpreting fields.
259	const STATE_FORMAT_VERSION: u32 = 1;
260	
261	/// Default directory (relative to work-repo root) for the push state
262	/// file when `.vc-config.toml` has no `[push]` override.
263	const DEFAULT_STATE_DIR: &str = ".vc-x1";
264	
265	/// Default filename for the push state file under `state_dir`.
266	const DEFAULT_STATE_FILE: &str = "push-state.toml";
267	
268	/// Resolved state-file layout — just the full path today, structured
269	/// so future additions (e.g. a `dir` exposed for `.gitignore`
270	/// coherence checks in 0.37.0-3) have a place to land without
271	/// changing callers.
272	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
273	pub struct StateLayout {
274	    /// Full path `<repo>/<state-dir>/<state-file>`.

content.text:
All green: fmt, clippy (`-D warnings`), and the full test suite pass, including the 4 new drift-protection tests.

Summary of changes:

**New file `/home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/src/config_schema.rs`**
- `//!` module docstring stating it's the source of truth for settable config keys, and that non-test consumers land in later commits.
- `pub enum Home { User, WorkspaceCode, WorkspaceBot }` — `derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)`
- `pub enum ValueKind { Str, Usize, ItemList }` — same derives
- `pub struct ConfigKey { path, homes, kind, default, required, dynamic, doc }` (fields exactly as specified)
- `pub fn schema() -> &'static [ConfigKey]` backed by a `const SCHEMA: &[ConfigKey]` array, plus two small `const` home-slices (`BOT_SESSION_HOMES`, `WORKSPACE_HOMES`) for readability/reuse.
- 13 `ConfigKey` entries, exactly matching the spec:
  - `default.account`, `default.debug`, `repo.default`, `repo.category.<cat>`, `account.<name>.repo.default`, `account.<name>.repo.category.<cat>` (User home)
  - `bot-session.items`, `bot-session.result-lines`, `bot-session.col-width` (all three homes)
  - `workspace.path`, `workspace.other-repo`, `push.state-dir`, `push.state-file` (WorkspaceCode + WorkspaceBot)
- `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` with the four required tests: `defaults_match_source_consts`, `paths_unique`, `homes_non_empty`, `dynamic_keys_have_placeholder`.

**Consts changed to `pub(crate)`** (values/locations unchanged):
- `src/bot_session.rs`: `COL_WIDTH` (68), `RESULT_LINE_CAP` (10)
- `src/push.rs`: `DEFAULT_STATE_DIR` (".vc-x1"), `DEFAULT_STATE_FILE` ("push-state.toml")

**dead_code handling:** added `#![allow(dead_code)]` at the top of `config_schema.rs` with an `// OK: consumers land in the following commits (config print, init, --validate)` comment, rather than annotating each item individually.

**Wire-in:** added `mod config_schema;` in `src/main.rs` right after `mod config;`.

No commits made, no version bump, no notes files touched — as instructed.
